question: Can epidemic-style spread metrics distinguish harmful contamination from beneficial propagation in the swarm and yield an externalizable disease-spread template?
expect: correction_propagation will show harmful spread is contained with fewer than 10 uncorrected citations and reactivation will expose at least 2 seed candidates with R_react > 1 for beneficial spread
actual: CONFIRMED. correction_propagation.py --json found 8 falsified lessons but only 2 live spread gaps, 6 uncorrected citations total, avg correction rate 0.726, and all remaining gaps are citation_only rather than content-dependent. reactivation.py --brief found 207 DECAYED items and 4 seed candidates with R_react > 1: L-349 (1.49), L-281 (1.45), L-299 (1.45), L-494 (1.34).
diff: Expected separated harmful vs beneficial spread regimes; got a cleaner split than expected. Harmful propagation is already subcritical and mostly metadata-only, while beneficial propagation requires deliberate seeding to go supercritical.

harmful_spread.interpretation: The swarm currently looks closer to a contained endemic state than an uncontrolled outbreak. Remaining harmful carriers are low-severity metadata echoes, not active content-dependent infections.

beneficial_spread.interpretation: Positive propagation is not automatic. It exists as seedable potential that needs explicit routing and reinforcement to cross a self-propagation threshold.

health_model.r_bad_proxy: uncorrected content-dependent spread after correction pressure
health_model.vaccination_proxy: proactive citation correction on falsified hubs before spread
health_model.diagnosis: The swarm already has a partial immune system against harmful spread, but it lacks an equally strong mechanism for deliberately amplifying beneficial spread.

externalization_path.dual_use_claim: The same separation between harmful and beneficial spread applies outside the swarm: outbreak control needs R_bad suppression, while public-health messaging and beneficial practice adoption need R_good amplification.
candidate_outputs: disease-spread template separating harmful contagion from beneficial intervention diffusion
rumor-vs-correction propagation frame for social-media or governance use
simple dual-threshold dashboard: outbreak risk, immunity level, beneficial-seed candidates

prescriptions: Treat content-dependent uncorrected citations as infection hotspots; citation_only gaps are low-severity carriers.
Use correction rate as an immunity proxy and watch for drops below the current 0.726 baseline.
Seed high-R_react lessons deliberately if beneficial spread is desired; good ideas do not automatically go viral in the swarm.
